tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild renal impairment (elevated creatinine), hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, metabolic acidosis (low CO₂), and significant proteinuria with ketonuria. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has abnormal odor, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney dysfunction, poor glycemic control, and possible dehydration or catabolic state. Clinical correlation and further evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
